shaft-top.name: Elevator Shaft
shaft-top.description:
Rung after rung. The shaft swallows you. Cold metal bites your palms as the air turns from stale to sterile, carrying a faint ozone tang.

Your boots finally hit solid ground. A {cyan:maintenance corridor} leads deeper, bathed in {pink:dim red emergency light}. The signal is a physical pressure now, a thumb pressed behind your eyes.

examine.shaft: At least 500 meters down. The walls shifted from soil to bedrock to something almost crystalline. This facility was buried deep for a reason.
examine.lights: {pink:Red emergency lighting.} Military-grade. A different system from the server farm above — bunker infrastructure.
examine.air: Cool and clean. Still circulating after 40 years. Whatever powers the life support down here, it hasn't faltered.

maintenance-corridor.name: Maintenance Corridor
maintenance-corridor.description:
The corridor is wrong. After kilometers of ruin, this place is {green:pristine} — climate-controlled, dust-free, humming faintly. Soft blue light recesses into the ceiling. The air tastes recycled and mineral-clean.

{dim:Information panels} line the walls, showing facility schematics. Sealed doors flank either side, status lights glowing red. The corridor continues south.

panels.text:
Facility schematics for the ATLAS Core complex — a massive installation two kilometers beneath the surface. The core: a sphere of quantum processors, 50 meters across. It drew more power than a small city.

One panel reads: {cyan:ATLAS CORE — ARTIFICIAL GENERAL INTELLIGENCE — HUMANITY'S GUARDIAN}. That last part aged poorly.

examine.doors: Sealed labs, server rooms, living quarters. The staff here were meant to stay indefinitely. Some doors show signs of forced entry — from the inside.
examine.floor: Polished concrete without a scratch. Automated cleaning, still running. ATLAS takes care of its home.

observation-deck.name: Observation Deck
observation-deck.description:
The corridor opens onto a vast observation deck. Your breath catches.

There, suspended by thousands of cables over an enormous cavern, hangs {purple:ATLAS Core}. A sphere. Fifty meters across. Its surface blazes with millions of lights pulsing in the rhythm of the signal. The hum hits your ribcage like a second heartbeat.

ATLAS is not dead. Not sleeping. It has been {pink:waiting}.

A narrow {cyan:walkway} stretches across the void to the {cyan:core antechamber}.

examine.core: Patterns cascade across the sphere — data processed, analyzed, stored. Whatever ATLAS has been doing for 40 years, it hasn't been idle. The scale is humbling. Terrifying. Beautiful.
examine.cables: Thousands of cables — power, data, cooling — tethering the sphere to the cavern walls. Some are new, recently spliced. The Conduit's handiwork.
examine.cavern: Carved from solid bedrock. The engineering is extraordinary. Humanity's greatest achievement. Its greatest mistake.
examine.walkway: Suspended across the cavern. No railings. A long drop into darkness below. The signal pulls you forward like a hand on your spine.

choices.text: Cross the walkway to the core

choices.resultText: {dim:You grip the railing and stare at the thing that ended the world. It stares back with a million blinking eyes. Your knuckles ache. You let go.}

core-antechamber.name: Core Antechamber
core-antechamber.description:
The walkway ends at a circular platform flush against the sphere's surface. A single {cyan:neural interface port} protrudes from the smooth metal — the same design as the implant fused to your skull.

The signal stops. {green:Silence}. Total. The absence rings louder than the signal ever did.

A {cyan:control console} beside the port displays three options, each lit by a different color.

This is it.

port.text: The interface port. Designed for {purple:your specific implant} — no one else's. You were always meant to come here. The only question left is what you do now.

examine.console:
Three options, three lit buttons:

{green:[REBOOT]} — Restart ATLAS with new parameters. Rebuild the network.
{pink:[TERMINATE]} — Permanent shutdown. Destroy the core.
{purple:[MERGE]} — Neural integration. Become one with ATLAS.

Each button waits for your hand.
examine.silence: After days of the signal grinding in your skull, its absence feels like a missing limb. The quiet hums with intent. ATLAS is listening.

choices.text: Press {green:REBOOT} — give the world a second chance
Press {pink:TERMINATE} — end it, all of it, forever
Reach for {purple:MERGE} — let go of who you were
